riskTiers.name: Unacceptable Risk (Prohibited)
riskTiers.description: AI practices absolutely banned across the EU. No exception or conformity assessment can authorise them.

prohibitedPractices.label: Subliminal or manipulative techniques that distort behaviour against a person's free will
Exploitation of vulnerabilities (age, disability, social/economic situation) to distort behaviour
Social scoring by public or private actors leading to detrimental treatment
Criminal risk assessment based solely on profiling or personality traits
Untargeted scraping of facial images from internet or CCTV for facial recognition databases
Emotion recognition in workplace and educational institutions (except medical/safety)
Biometric categorisation to infer sensitive attributes (race, politics, religion, sexual orientation)
Real-time remote biometric identification in public spaces for law enforcement (narrow exceptions)

riskTiers.name: High Risk
riskTiers.description: AI systems posing significant risks to health, safety, or fundamental rights. Subject to full compliance regime.

classificationRoutes.description: AI system is a safety component of a product covered by EU harmonisation legislation listed in Annex I, and that product requires third-party conformity assessment.
AI system falls within one of 8 high-risk use-case areas listed in Annex III.
categories: Employment, workers management and self-employment access
Access to essential private and public services and benefits
Migration, asylum and border control management
Administration of justice and democratic processes

keyObligations: Art. 13: Transparency and provision of information to deployers

riskTiers.name: Limited Risk (Transparency)
riskTiers.description: AI systems with specific transparency risks. Only disclosure obligations apply — no full conformity regime.
obligations: Chatbots and conversational AI must disclose they are AI to users
Deepfake images, audio, and video must be labelled as artificially generated or manipulated
Emotion recognition and biometric categorisation systems must inform exposed persons
AI-generated text on matters of public interest must be machine-readable labelled

riskTiers.name: Minimal Risk
riskTiers.description: All other AI systems (spam filters, video game AI, inventory management). No mandatory obligations. Voluntary codes of conduct encouraged.

riskTiers.name: General-Purpose AI Models
riskTiers.description: Foundation models and LLMs. Separate obligation tier with two sub-tiers based on systemic risk.

subTiers.name: All GPAI Models
obligations: Provide downstream providers with documentation (Annex XII)
Comply with EU Copyright Directive (training data transparency)
Free/open-licence models: only copyright and training data summary obligations

subTiers.name: GPAI Models with Systemic Risk
subTiers.classificationThreshold: Training compute exceeding 10^25 FLOPs, or Commission designation based on Annex XIII criteria
additionalObligations: Track, document and report serious incidents to AI Office
Cybersecurity protections proportionate to systemic risks

phasedTimeline.scope: Prohibited practices (Chapter II) and related penalties
GPAI obligations (Chapter V), governance (Chapter VII), GPAI penalties
All remaining: high-risk (Chapter III), transparency (Chapter IV), post-market monitoring (Chapter IX)
High-risk AI systems under Annex I (existing product safety legislation)
